description: Add a Remove button to FailedPluginCard so users can remove broken local plugin paths from config. Currently, when a local plugin path in config.json doesn't exist or fails to load, the side panel shows a red error card with no way to dismiss it or remove the stale entry. The only recourse is manually editing ~/.opentabs/config.json. This adds a full removal flow: a new server-side JSON-RPC method that removes a localPlugins entry by its raw specifier string, a new bridge function and background message handler, and a Remove button with confirmation dialog on FailedPluginCard — following the exact design pattern used by PluginMenu's existing remove flow.

userStories.title: Add server-side removeLocalPluginBySpecifier and plugin.removeBySpecifier JSON-RPC method
userStories.description: Add a new function removeLocalPluginBySpecifier in plugin-management.ts that removes a localPlugins entry by matching the raw specifier string (exact string match against the config array), and a new handlePluginRemoveBySpecifier handler in extension-handlers.ts that dispatches plugin.removeBySpecifier requests. This is needed because the existing removePlugin/removeLocalPlugin path requires a plugin name and a registry lookup — failed plugins never enter the registry so they can't be removed that way.
acceptanceCriteria: removeLocalPluginBySpecifier(state, specifier) removes the exact string from config.json localPlugins array and returns true, or returns false if not found
plugin.removeBySpecifier JSON-RPC method accepts { specifier: string }, calls removeLocalPluginBySpecifier, triggers onReload, and sends plugins.changed notification
plugin.removeBySpecifier returns an error if specifier is empty or not found in config
The extension-protocol.ts dispatch map routes 'plugin.removeBySpecifier' to the new handler
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/plugin-management.ts (add removeLocalPluginBySpecifier function)
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts (add handlePluginRemoveBySpecifier handler, export it)
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-protocol.ts (add dispatch route)

Context:
The existing removal path works by plugin *name*: `removePlugin(name, state, onReload)` at line 471 calls `findPlugin(state, name)` which searches `state.registry.plugins` — but failed plugins never enter the registry. The existing `removeLocalPlugin(state, pluginName)` at line 378 iterates `localPlugins` paths and tries to match by directory basename or package.json name — this doesn't work for broken paths where package.json can't be read.

For failed plugins, we only have the raw specifier string from config.json (e.g., `../plugins/gmail`, `~/dev/my-plugin`). The simplest correct approach is exact string matching against `config.localPlugins`.

Changes:
1. In plugin-management.ts, add a new function `removeLocalPluginBySpecifier`:
```typescript
const removeLocalPluginBySpecifier = async (
  state: { configWriteMutex: Promise<void> },
  specifier: string,
): Promise<boolean> => {
```
This should use the same read-modify-write pattern as `removeLocalPlugin` (lines 378-457) — serialized via `configWriteMutex`, read config, filter out the matching specifier from `localPlugins`, write back with `atomicWrite`. Use exact string match: `localPlugins.filter(p => p !== specifier)`.

Export the new function.

2. In extension-handlers.ts, add `handlePluginRemoveBySpecifier` following the exact pattern of `handlePluginRemove` (lines 754-788):
```typescript
const handlePluginRemoveBySpecifier = async (
  state: ServerState,
  params: Record<string, unknown> | undefined,
  id: string | number,
  callbacks: McpCallbacks,
): Promise<void> => {
```
Validate `params.specifier` is a non-empty string. Call `removeLocalPluginBySpecifier(state, specifier)`. If not found, send error code -32602. On success, call `callbacks.onReload()`, send `plugins.changed` notification via `buildConfigStatePayload`, then send success result. Note: do NOT call `callbacks.queryExtension('plugin.uninstall', ...)` — failed plugins have no adapter to uninstall.

Export `handlePluginRemoveBySpecifier`.

3. In extension-protocol.ts, add dispatch route. Look at line 428-431 for the existing pattern:
```typescript
if (method === 'plugin.remove' && id !== undefined) {
  void handlePluginRemove(state, params, id, callbacks);
  return;
}
```
Add a similar block for 'plugin.removeBySpecifier' routing to `handlePluginRemoveBySpecifier`.

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify the existing `removePlugin` or `removeLocalPlugin` functions — they work correctly for successfully loaded plugins.
- Do NOT add a registry lookup — the whole point is that failed plugins aren't in the registry.
- The specifier match must be exact string equality against config.json's localPlugins entries. Do not resolve or normalize paths — the specifier in the FailedPluginCard comes directly from config.json via discoveryErrors.

userStories.title: Add extension bridge and background message handler for removeFailedPlugin
userStories.description: Add a new bridge function removeFailedPlugin(specifier) and a new background message type bg:removeFailedPlugin that sends a plugin.removeBySpecifier JSON-RPC request to the server. This connects the side panel UI to the server-side removal path added in US-001.
acceptanceCriteria: bridge.ts exports removeFailedPlugin(specifier: string) that sends bg:removeFailedPlugin message and returns Promise<{ ok: true }>
BgRemoveFailedPluginMessage type is defined in extension-messages.ts with type 'bg:removeFailedPlugin' and specifier field
Background message handler sends plugin.removeBySpecifier JSON-RPC with { specifier } to the server
The handler is registered in the backgroundHandlers dispatch map
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/bridge.ts (add removeFailedPlugin function)
- platform/browser-extension/src/extension-messages.ts (add BgRemoveFailedPluginMessage type)
- platform/browser-extension/src/background-message-handlers.ts (add handler + register in map)
- platform/browser-extension/src/offscreen/index.ts (add case for bg:removeFailedPlugin in the relay switch)

Context:
The existing pattern for plugin removal:
- bridge.ts line 142-143: `const removePlugin = (name: string): Promise<{ ok: true }> => sendBgMessage<{ ok: true }>({ type: 'bg:removePlugin', name });`
- extension-messages.ts line 162-165: `interface BgRemovePluginMessage { type: 'bg:removePlugin'; name: string; }`
- background-message-handlers.ts line 560-570: `handleBgRemovePlugin` sends `sendServerRequest('plugin.remove', { name })`
- The handler is registered at line 607: `['bg:removePlugin', handleBgRemovePlugin]`

Changes:
1. In extension-messages.ts, add after line 165 (after BgRemovePluginMessage):
```typescript
/** Side panel → Background: remove a failed plugin by its config specifier */
export interface BgRemoveFailedPluginMessage {
  type: 'bg:removeFailedPlugin';
  specifier: string;
}
```
Also add `BgRemoveFailedPluginMessage` to the `InternalMessage` union type.

2. In bridge.ts, add after the existing `removePlugin` (line 143):
```typescript
const removeFailedPlugin = (specifier: string): Promise<{ ok: true }> =>
  sendBgMessage<{ ok: true }>({ type: 'bg:removeFailedPlugin', specifier });
```
Export it.

3. In background-message-handlers.ts, add a new handler following the pattern of `handleBgRemovePlugin` (lines 560-570):
```typescript
const handleBgRemoveFailedPlugin: MessageHandler = (message, sendResponse) => {
  const specifier = message.specifier as string;
  sendServerRequest('plugin.removeBySpecifier', { specifier })
    .then((result: unknown) => { sendResponse(result); })
    .catch((err: unknown) => { sendResponse({ error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err) }); });
};
```
Register it in the `backgroundHandlers` map after the existing `bg:removePlugin` entry (line 607).

4. In offscreen/index.ts, find the switch statement that relays background messages. Add `case 'bg:removeFailedPlugin':` to the existing set of cases that get relayed. Look at line 508 (`case 'bg:removePlugin':`) for the pattern.

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify the existing `removePlugin` bridge function or `handleBgRemovePlugin` handler.
- The new function takes `specifier` (not `name`) to distinguish it from the existing removal path.

userStories.title: Add Remove button with confirmation dialog to FailedPluginCard
userStories.description: Add a Remove button to FailedPluginCard that opens a confirmation dialog, then calls removeFailedPlugin(specifier) on confirm. The button and dialog must follow the existing PluginMenu removal pattern (destructive-styled confirmation dialog with red header). Wire it through PluginList and App.tsx.
acceptanceCriteria: FailedPluginCard shows a Remove button (Trash2 icon) in the top-right corner of the card
Clicking Remove opens a confirmation dialog with destructive styling matching PluginMenu's remove dialog
The dialog body shows the specifier path and explains the action will remove the path from config
Confirming calls removeFailedPlugin(specifier) and shows a loading state while removal is in progress
After successful removal, the card disappears (server sends plugins.changed which updates failedPlugins)
On error, a dismissible error message appears on the card
Storybook stories are updated to cover the new Remove button, loading state, and confirmation dialog
